posts.text:
Next time you see someone mention that shitty Van Rooj 2024 paper purporting to prove that AGI is intractable, point em at this.

TLDR: by Van Rooj's reasoning: ImageNet is intractable. Reductio ad Absurdum. Quod Erat Demonstrandum.
> Muon succeeds not by tracking an ideal global geometry, but by guaranteeing step-size optimality.

> we construct explicit, computable straight-line processes for arbitrary Gaussian endpoints... show that straight-line processes do not exist for targets with sufficiently well-separated modes... provide a structural theory of when straight-line generative flows can, and cannot, exist.

This would constitute one of the most brazen, most appalling acts of corruption in U.S. history.

I know we’re numb to all of this but this is a seven-alarm fire.

"reversed every single cut" is bullshit though. These grants went unfunded for a year. I imagine a lot of people were forced to abandon their research/degree and are now committed to other work/projects.

You can't just call "mulligan" on damages like this. "Reversing" the cuts isn't good enough.

What has me worried — what we all need to focus on — is that something important in our democracy broke last week, and we are only beginning to see the fallout. www.democracydocket.com/opinion/the-...

posts.text:
Georgia's governor signs a comically transparent law.

It makes local elections in 5 big blue counties nonpartisan, to give GOP candidates a chance to win without a party label in places like Atlanta.

And it doesn't change anything in the rest of the state, for red counties to know who's GOP.

the constitution and the law are living documents. the current court corrupts both by ignoring their own reasoning and precedent.

there either is rule of law, or there isn't.

reply_context.text: But more than that, it may be an issue about modern role of the Constitution. The Supreme Court's power waxed as the Constitution came to dominate law, and Congress as the lawmaking body retreated. Our attitude towards the Supreme Court in some ways mirrors our attitude towards the Constitution.

posts.text: It's feasible that this population is biased towards paying for services to have more control over their data/privacy, so the dataset might even be under-representative. It's also feasible that they self-select *into* this group for the anonymity.

reply_context.text: You can see this in the Wildchat dataset (researchers gave free ChatGPT access in exchange for users’ chats). It surely has its own biases, but it’s 4 million conversations that we can actually study outside of the companies: huggingface.co/datasets/all...
